title: aspidosperma macrocarpon
intro: Aspidosperma macrocarpon, also known as the Pau Pereira tree, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with A. macrocarpon include pain relief, reduced fever, and improved wound healing. A. macrocarpon is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used as a topical application to treat skin conditions and wounds.
